2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch adds a new function called untagged_addr_remote_unlocked() to the x86/mm module. The function is used to look up the VMA of an address without taking the mmap lock, which can be contended by other tasks on large machines. This is done by annotating access to mm->context.untag_mask with READ_ONCE() and WRITE_ONCE() to make existing lockless reads explicit and KCSAN-clean. The patch also updates several functions in the arch/x86/include/asm/mmu_context.h, arch/x86/include/asm/uaccess_64.h, and include/linux/uaccess.h files to use the new function or update their behavior accordingly.

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Requested that Acked-by be changed to include their new email address in the next revision.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, 17 Jul 2026 13:00:31 -0400 Rik van Riel <riel@surriel.com> wrote:

> __access_remote_vm() reads another process's memory under the mmap lock. On
> large machines that lock is contended by tasks polling /proc/PID/cmdline,
> /proc/PID/environ, or calling process_vm_readv(), even though the memory
> they read is almost always resident and could be reached under the per-VMA
> lock instead.
> 
> Looking up the VMA first requires untagging the address.
> untagged_addr_remote() asserts the mmap lock only because it reads
> mm->context.untag_mask, which can race with the write in mm_enable_lam().
> 
> That mask is set once, when LAM is enabled, and never changes afterwards,
> so the read itself does not need the lock. It is already read without it,
> from the context switch path and /proc/PID/status.
> 
> Add untagged_addr_remote_unlocked() for callers that have not taken the
> mmap lock, and annotate access to mm->context.untag_mask with READ_ONCE()
> and WRITE_ONCE() so the existing lockless reads are explicit and
> KCSAN-clean. untagged_addr_remote() keeps its assertion and shares the same
> code.
> 
> Assisted-by: Claude:claude-opus-4.8
> Acked-by: Usama Arif <usamaarif642@gmail.com>

Hi Rik,

Would you mind changing this to

Acked-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>

in the next revision

Thanks!

2026-07-20.patch_summary: The patch adds a new function, untagged_addr_remote_unlocked(), to allow reading another process's memory under the per-VMA lock without taking the mmap lock. This is done by annotating access to mm->context.untag_mask with READ_ONCE() and WRITE_ONCE() to make existing lockless reads explicit and KCSAN-clean.
